title: Mullin Takes the Algarve
title_candidates: First Blood in Portugal: Mullin Sets the Standard
theme: David Mullin sets a new standard for TEG: a coronation on a new continent, bookended by the two best rounds ever played, with Jon Baker the only man who briefly looked like he could live with him.
opening_hook: Open on Mullin's closing -2 in R3 — the best round in TEG history — then flash back to how Boavista was conquered, because the headline is the record-shattering, not the build-up.
foreshadow: Boavista's back nine — and the 14th in particular — as a serial wrecker of cards
Jon Baker as the one man capable of pressing Mullin, and the fragility that would betray him
Henry Meller's slide to the bottom of the Spoon race as early as R1 H3

competitions.name: Trophy (Net VP)
competitions.how: Mullin led from R1 on the back of a record +0, was overhauled by Jon Baker after R2 when a closing 16-18 collapse (b48) cost him 10 shots, then reclaimed the lead for good at R3 H3 (b16) and pulled away with the greatest round in TEG history (b03) to win by 7 at +6.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Never headed after a record-setting R1 +20 (b02), Mullin extended through R2 and closed with another record gross round of +18 (b04) to win by 11 at +66 over Stuart Neumann (b14). A procession.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Meller fell to the bottom as early as R1 H3 (b49) and never climbed out, his card shredded by a brutal back nine (b21, b20) and a R2 13-16 bleed (b50). He did, in fairness, post the 2nd-best gross round in TEG history in R3 (b09) — too late to matter.

headline_candidates: Mullin Sets a Marker, Boavista Sets a Trap
A New Course, A New Benchmark
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin Sets a Marker, Boavista Sets a Trap
rounds.angle: Mullin's record-breaking +0 / +20 gross opens TEG2 on a new course, while the back nine — and the par-3 14th in particular — devours the rest of the field.
Jon Baker briefly seizes the Trophy lead with a +1 — the day's best net round — as Mullin haemorrhages 10 shots over 16-18; but the Jacket lead never moves.

headline_candidates: The Best Round Ever Played in TEG
rounds.angle: Baker bleeds 12 shots out of the gate, Mullin retakes the lead at H3 and never looks back, closing with a -2 that is both the best net and best gross round in TEG history.

players.arc: Untouchable: a record +0 to open, the only wobble at the end of R2, then a record-breaking -2 to close and the Trophy/Jacket double by daylight.
The only credible challenger — two top-class rounds and a brief R2 lead — undone by a R3 opening five-hole bleed and a habit of finding 8s.
Quietly excellent in gross terms (Jacket runner-up, two top-three gross rounds), but the 14th and a R1 13-17 wobble stopped the net challenge.
Bottom from R1 H3 to the final putt; salvaged pride with the 2nd-best gross round in TEG history in R3 — far too late.
A serial blow-up artist: a 9 at the 14th, a 9 at the 17th, a 9 at R3's 12th, and a 22-shot R1 back-nine collapse to set the tone.

venue_notes: TEG's first visit to the Algarve and a brand-new course in Boavista (Howard Swan parkland, Atlantic views, Lagos), played three times in three days — so frame it as a course the field had to learn in real time, with the back nine (especially the par-3 14th) emerging as the defining stretch.
